text: Confirm the problem: `sudo -u oncall sudo -n id` → 'oncall is not in the sudoers file'. You need to grant oncall sudo rights.

The right way is a drop-in under `/etc/sudoers.d/` (not editing `/etc/sudoers` by hand). Create `/etc/sudoers.d/oncall` containing: `oncall ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL`. Files there must have mode 0440 and no syntax errors.

Create it safely with visudo so a typo can't lock everyone out: `sudo visudo -f /etc/sudoers.d/oncall`, add the line, save. (Or `echo 'oncall ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL' | sudo tee /etc/sudoers.d/oncall && sudo chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/oncall && sudo visudo -c`.) Verify `sudo -u oncall sudo -n id`.
